skill.name: weekly-report
skill.description: Helps users turn scattered weekly work notes into a clear weekly report with outcomes, blockers, and next steps.
skill.summary: Organize weekly work updates into structured, honest, and action-focused reports.
skill.author: yunshu0909

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak cryptography, and high entropy indicators, but review found these are false positives in markdown examples, inline file paths, and Chinese text. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, network access, or executable code was found in SKILL.md. The only residual concern is that the skill suggests saving a generated report file, which is normal for this workflow and should stay under user control.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Markdown Code Fence
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution at a markdown code fence. The line starts an example report template and does not execute code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a markdown fence before a sample report layout. No command, interpreter call, or user-controlled execution path is present.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Markdown Code Fence
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution at the closing markdown fence. The line only closes the sample report template.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a markdown delimiter, not executable syntax. The surrounding content is a document outline.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Inline Filename
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged backticks around a suggested filename. The content is a markdown-formatted output filename, not a shell command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks format a filename in text. There is no command substitution, Ruby execution, or instruction to run the value.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Example Directory Path
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged an example macOS document path. The line is guidance for where a user may store the report.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The path is shown as documentation and contains no command syntax. It does imply file saving, but not unsafe execution.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Inline Folder Name
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged backticks around an example folder name. The content is a storage suggestion for generated reports.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line formats a directory name in markdown. It does not request command execution or dynamic evaluation.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Dialogue Code Fence
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged another markdown code fence. The fenced block contains an example assistant and user conversation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The fenced block is conversational documentation. It contains no executable code, shell commands, or hidden instructions.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography in the description line. Review found no cryptographic algorithm or security-sensitive operation on that line.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is Chinese frontmatter describing the weekly report skill. No hash, cipher, key handling, or crypto library reference appears there.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: High Entropy Content
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged high entropy at the file start. Review found normal markdown frontmatter and Chinese prose, not binary or encrypted content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file begins with plain YAML frontmatter and readable text. The entropy result is consistent with multilingual UTF-8 content, not obfuscation.

content.value_statement: Weekly reports are often hard to write because work notes are scattered and missing context. This skill guides users through collection, organization, and revision to produce a clear report.
actual_capabilities: Collects loose weekly work notes through guided questions.
Organizes work into role-specific modules such as development, product, operations, design, QA, or sales support.
Prompts for background, actions, results, blockers, status, and next steps.
Creates a structured weekly report with completed work and next week priorities.
Helps preserve the user's tone while improving clarity and logic.
Encourages honest reporting of achievements, risks, and responsibility boundaries.
limitations: Does not verify whether reported work or metrics are accurate.
Requires the user to provide enough context for meaningful summaries.
Does not replace manager review for sensitive or high-stakes reports.
File saving should be confirmed by the user in tools that can write files.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Team Status Report
use_cases.description: Turn informal notes into a weekly report with progress, blockers, and next actions.

use_cases.title: Summarize Cross-Functional Work
use_cases.description: Group work across projects, customers, meetings, and internal tasks for clearer communication.

use_cases.title: Review Work Before Submission
use_cases.description: Check whether a draft report has clear context, measurable outcomes, and realistic next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Weekly Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me write my weekly report. Ask me questions first, then organize my answers into a clear draft.

prompt_templates.title: Organize by Work Module
prompt_templates.prompt: I worked on several unrelated tasks this week. Help me classify them into report sections and identify missing details.

prompt_templates.title: Strengthen Results and Blockers
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my weekly report draft. Make the results, blockers, current status, and next steps clearer without exaggerating.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Role-Specific Report
prompt_templates.scenario: Write for a specific job function.
prompt_templates.prompt: I am a product manager. Help me create a weekly report that covers research, design, reviews, feedback, and next week priorities.

output_examples.input: I fixed two bugs, joined a review meeting, and started a customer dashboard task.
output_examples.output: A report section that separates bug fixes, collaboration, and dashboard work, with prompts for impact and next steps.

output_examples.input: My report feels too vague and does not show value.
output_examples.output: A revised draft that adds background, concrete outcomes, current status, and honest blockers where details are available.

output_examples.input: I need next week priorities based on this week's unresolved items.
output_examples.output: A short next-week plan with priority items, responsible owners, and expected follow-up actions.

best_practices: Provide raw notes first, even if they are not organized.
Include numbers, dates, customer names, and status details when they are safe to share.
Review the draft for tone, accuracy, and responsibility boundaries before submitting it.
anti_patterns: Do not ask the skill to invent results or metrics.
Do not hide important blockers that affect delivery or planning.
Do not submit generated text without checking facts and sensitive details.

faq.question: What does this skill help me produce?
faq.answer: It helps produce a weekly work report with completed work, results, blockers, and next week priorities.

faq.question: Can it write reports for different roles?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for development, product, operations, design, QA, sales support, and general work.

faq.question: Does it require perfect notes?
faq.answer: No. It is designed to collect scattered notes first, then organize them into a coherent structure.

faq.question: Will it exaggerate my work?
faq.answer: No. The instructions emphasize honest reporting, clear limits, and accurate responsibility boundaries.

faq.question: Can it save the report as a file?
faq.answer: It suggests a filename and folder, but file writing depends on the host tool and user approval.

faq.question: Is it only for Chinese weekly reports?
faq.answer: The source instructions are Chinese, but the workflow can support reports in other languages if requested.
